name: Jamaican Jerked Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Make Pickapeppa sauce (see note).
2. Cut away rind and most fat from pork; remove all meat from bones, cut into pieces about 1-1/2 inches thick and 3 to 4 inches long. Put pork pieces and chicken in a large bowl, cover and refrigerate until needed.
3. To prepare seasoning, heat the allspice berries over medium heat in small saucepan for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring often; crush 2 tablespoons at a time in mortar and pestle.
4. In a medium bowl, combine crushed berries, nutmeg, cinnamon, scallions, garlic, pepper, and 1 tablespoon vinegar; crush into pasty mixture.
5. Add the remaining vinegar, oil, salt, pepper, and bay leaves. Turn the mixture over pork and chicken and rub meat with seasoning, coating evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
6. Cook jerked pork and chicken on grill over hot coals or on gas barbecue as far from heat as possible (at least 6 inches). Cover with lid; turn meat every 10 minutes for about 1 hour until done.
7. If desired, halfway through cooking time, toss 1 teaspoon whole allspice berries into the fire.
8. Cut cooked pork into 1/3 inch slices and serve with chicken. Accompany with Pickapeppa sauce. Serves 6 to 8.
(Note: The jerked meat can also be cooked in a preheated 350°F oven on a rack in a roasting pan for about 1 hour, turning once, though it will not have the same grill flavor.)
